leadText: Nuzzle (Japanese: ほっぺすりすり Cheek Nuzzle) is a damage-dealing Electric-type move introduced in Generation VI.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] Nuzzle inflicts damage and paralyzes its target when it hits. Nuzzle can also be used as part of a Contest Spectacular combination , with the user gaining an extra three appeal points if the move Charge was used in the prior turn. It can also be used to start a combination, causing Hex and Smelling Salts to give extra three appeal points if either of them is used in the next turn. Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A Category Physical Accuracy 100% Standard Plus Move Power 20 24 Cooldown 6s Move wind-up time 10 frames Effect duration —s Additional effect — — — None

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA S V The user attacks by nuzzling its electrified cheeks against the target. This also leaves the target with paralysis. ZA The user attacks by nuzzling targets with its electrified cheeks. This leaves targets paralyzed.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description S MD It damages an enemy. It could also cause paralysis.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Nuzzle is the only Electric-type Cute move. The majority of Pokémon that learn Nuzzle are the electric rodents (except for Morpeko ), and it used to be exclusive to them until Pokémon Sword and Shield . Hatenna is the only non-Electric-type Pokémon that can learn Nuzzle.
